heading: Definition and nonapplication
label: 20301 Definition and nonapplication
notes: Historical and Revision Notes. 103–272Pub. LRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)20301(a)45:8 (“trains, locomotives, tenders, cars, and similar vehicles”).45:9 (3d sentence).20301(b)45:6 (1st sentence proviso).(1st sentence proviso), ; restated , .Mar. 2, 1893, ch. 196, § 627 Stat. 532Apr. 1, 1896, ch. 8729 Stat. 8545:8 (words after 16th comma).(words after 23d comma), .Mar. 2, 1903, ch. 976, § 132 Stat. 943Subsection (a) is added to avoid repeating the substance of the definition throughout this chapter.In subsection (b), the words before clause (1) are substituted for “, That nothing in sections 1 to 7 of this title shall apply to” in 45:6 because 45:9, 11, and 16 provide that 45:9 and 11–16 apply to the same vehicles and trains as 45:1–7 apply to. In clause (1), the word “coal” is added for clarity because of the decision of the Supreme Court in . v. , 353 U.S. 325, 333 (1957) and the legislative history of 45:6 (proviso). See 24 Cong. Rec. 1477 (1893). The text of 45:8 (words after last comma) is omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “railroad” in section 20102 of the revised title.ProvidedBaltimore & Ohio Railway CoJackson
Pub. L. 104–287This amends 49:20301(b) to clarify the restatement of 45:8 by (, ).section 1 of the Act of July 5, 1994Public Law 103–272108 Stat. 881

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: a .—Definition
children.text: In this chapter, “vehicle” means a car, locomotive, tender, or similar vehicle.
a train of 8-wheel standard logging cars if the height of each car from the top of the rail to the center of the coupling is not more than 25 inches.
a locomotive used in hauling a train referred to in clause (2) of this subsection when the locomotive and cars of the train are used only to transport logs.
a car, locomotive, or train used on a street railway.
